# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }
truefix-session = { path = "crates/truefix-session" }
truefix-transport = { path = "crates/truefix-transport" }
truefix-config = { path = "crates/truefix-config" }
truefix-store = { path = "crates/truefix-store" }
truefix-log = { path = "crates/truefix-log" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.
opt-level = 3

- `tags`: constants SOH/BEGIN_STRING(8)/BODY_LENGTH(9)/MSG_TYPE(35)/CHECK_SUM(10)/MSG_SEQ_NUM(34)/SENDER_COMP_ID(49)/TARGET_COMP_ID(56)/SENDING_TIME(52); `is_header(tag)`, `is_trailer(tag)`, `data_field_for_length(tag)->Option<u32>` (maps length→data tag pairs incl. 95→96, 90→91, 212→213, 34x→34x+1 encoded-* pairs).
- `codec::encode` — canonical order `8,9,35,<rest header>,<body>,<trailer except 10>,10`; computes BodyLength + CheckSum (mod 256); recursively renders groups (count tag = entries.len()).
- `codec::decode` — tokenizes `tag=value<SOH>` honoring length-prefixed binary data fields; verifies BeginString/BodyLength/CheckSum; routes fields to header/body/trailer via `is_header`/`is_trailer`. **Fully implemented, flat-field (no dict group parsing at this layer).**

# 2. truefix-dict (dual-track data dictionary)
Files: `src/{lib,model,parser,validate,fixt,hash}.rs`, `build.rs`, `dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict` (FIX40/41/42/43/44, FIXT11, FIX50/50SP1/50SP2).
**Public types & API:**
- `FieldType` enum {Int,Length,SeqNum,NumInGroup,Float,Price,Qty,Amt,Percentage,Char,Boolean,String,Data,UtcTimestamp,UtcTimeOnly,UtcDateOnly,MonthYear} — `parse(token)`, `value_ok(&Field)->bool`.
- `FieldDef { tag, name, field_type, values: Vec<String> }` — `allows(&str)->bool`.
- `MessageDef { msg_type, name, required: Vec<u32>, optional: Vec<u32> }` — `allows_tag(u32)`.
- `DataDictionary` (fields private `pub(crate)`: version, fields, field_by_name, messages, header, trailer, hash) — accessors `version()`, `hash()`, `field(tag)`, `field_by_name(name)`, `message(msg_type)`, `is_header/is_trailer(tag)`, `field_count/message_count`.
- `RejectReason` enum (codes: InvalidTagNumber=0, RequiredTagMissing=1, TagNotDefinedForMessageType=2, UndefinedTag=3, TagSpecifiedWithoutValue=4, ValueIsIncorrect=5, IncorrectDataFormat=6, InvalidMsgType=11) — `code()->u32`.
- `ValidationError { reason, ref_tag, business: bool, text }` (thiserror) — `session`/`business` constructors are `pub(crate)`.
- `ValidationOptions { validate_fields_have_values=true, validate_user_defined_fields=false, allow_unknown_msg_fields=false, check_required_fields=true, check_field_types=true }` — maps to FIX `Validate*` toggles.
- `FixtDictionaries { transport, applications: BTreeMap, default_appl_ver_id }` — `new`, `with_application`, `with_default_appl_ver_id`, `transport()`, `application_for(Option<&str>)`.
- `parser::parse(&str)->Result<DataDictionary, ParseError>` — parses directives `version`, `field`, `header`, `trailer`, `message` (with `req:`/`opt:` tag lists); `ParseError` {MissingVersion, Malformed{line,reason}, UnknownType{line,token}}. Comments via `#`. **Fully implemented.**
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate(&Message, &ValidationOptions)->Result<(), ValidationError>` — checks empty values, UDF handling (tags≥5000), field-type/format/enum, tag-membership, required-fields; returns session-level reject vs business-level reject (InvalidMsgType). **Fully implemented.**
- `hash.rs` + `build.rs`: FNV-1a 64-bit (duplicated in both for codegen/runtime parity). `build.rs` generates `generated.rs` with `*_DICT_HASH: u64` constants + `pub mod <version>_msgs { pub const MSGNAME: &str }` per version, scanned from `message` directives.
- `lib.rs` re-exports + `include!(generated.rs)` + bundled dict sources (`FIX40_DICT`..`FIX50SP2_DICT` const strs) + `load_fixXX()` fns + `ALL_DICTS` table. **Fully implemented, no stubs.**

# 3. truefix-session (sans-IO state machine)
Files: `src/{lib,state,config,schedule,admin,tags,application,time_util,session_id}.rs`.
**Public types & API:**
- `SessionState` enum {Disconnected, AwaitingLogon, LoggedOn, AwaitingLogout}.
- `Event` enum {Connected, Received(Message), Tick, StartLogout}.
- `Action` enum {Send(Message), Disconnect}.
- `SessionStatus { state, logged_on, next_sender_seq, next_target_seq }` (monitoring snapshot, FR-L1).
- `Session` (struct, private fields incl. `store: BTreeMap<u64,Message>` in-memory sent msgs, `queue: BTreeMap<u64,Message>` out-of-order, `validator: Option<(DataDictionary, ValidationOptions)>`). Methods: `new(config)`, `set_dictionary(dict, opts)`, `id()`, `state()`, `is_logged_on()`, `next_out_seq/next_in_seq()`, `seed_sequences(out,in)`, `status()`, `reset()`, `handle(Event)->Vec<Action>`, `send_app(Message)->Vec<Action>`. **Heavily implemented (S3):** logon (incl. ResetSeqNumFlag + NextExpectedMsgSeqNum 789), heartbeat/test-request, logout, latency check (CheckLatency/MaxLatency → logout on stale SendingTime), inbound seq validation (Equal/Greater[queue+ResendRequest]/Less[PossDup ignore or logout]), sequence-number accounting on Logon, ResendRequest (35=2) handling with chunked `resend_request_chunk_size`, SequenceReset (35=4) gap-fill vs reset, `drain_queue`, app-message validation hook producing Reject (35=3) / BusinessMessageReject (35=j), `prepare_resend` stamps PossDup+OrigSendingTime. Admin types 0/1/2/3/4/5/A excluded from validation.
- `admin.rs` (pub(crate)): builders `logon/logout/heartbeat/test_request/resend_request/sequence_reset/reject/reject_with_reason/business_message_reject/prepare_resend` — **fully implemented.**
- `config.rs`: `Role` enum {Initiator, Acceptor}; `SessionConfig` struct with fields: begin_string, sender_comp_id, target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval(=30), role, reset_on_logon(=true), reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size(=0), enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, check_latency(=true), max_latency(=120), logon_timeout(=10), logout_timeout(=10), reconnect_interval(=5), schedule: Option<Schedule>. `new()` + `session_id()`.
- `schedule.rs`: `Schedule { non_stop, start_time: Option<Time>, end_time, weekdays: Option<Vec<Weekday>>, utc_offset_seconds }` — `non_stop()`, `daily(start,end)`, `with_weekdays`, `with_utc_offset_seconds`, `is_in_session(now_utc)` (handles midnight wrap). **Implemented (simplified TZ, no StartDay/EndDay yet).**
- `application.rs`: `Application` async trait (on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app) with no-op defaults; from_admin/from_app return `Result<(),String>`.
- `session_id.rs`: `SessionId` (begin_string, sender/target comp+sub+location ids, session_qualifier) — `new()`, `Display` as `begin:sender->target[:qualifier]`.
- `tags.rs` (pub(crate)): MSG_SEQ_NUM(34), POSS_DUP_FLAG(43), SENDING_TIME(52), ORIG_SENDING_TIME(122), NEW_SEQ_NO(36), GAP_FILL_FLAG(123), BEGIN_SEQ_NO(7), END_SEQ_NO(16), RESET_SEQ_NUM_FLAG(141), NEXT_EXPECTED_MSG_SEQ_NUM(789), TEST_REQ_ID(112), TEXT(58), REF_SEQ_NUM(45), REF_MSG_TYPE(372), SESSION_REJECT_REASON(373), BUSINESS_REJECT_REASON(380), ENCRYPT_METHOD(98), HEART_BT_INT(108).
- Inline tests in `time_util.rs`: `format_has_expected_shape`.

# 4. truefix-transport (tokio TCP/TLS initiator+acceptor)
Files: `src/{lib,framing}.rs`.
**Public types & API:**
- `SocketOptions { tcp_no_delay }` — `apply(&TcpStream)`.
- `Services { store: Option<Arc<dyn MessageStore>>, log: Option<Arc<dyn Log>>, socket_options, monitor: Option<Monitor> }` (Clone/Default).
- `Monitor` (FR-L): `new`, `status(id)`, `is_connected(id)`, `sessions()`, `force_logout(id)`, `reset(id)` (async). Internal `MonitorEntry { status, connected, control }`.
- `SessionHandle { control, task }` — `logout()`, `join()`.
- `Acceptor<A>` (single static config) — `bind/bind_with`, `local_addr()`, `serve()->JoinHandle`.
- `AcceptorBuilder<A>` (multi-session) — `bind`, `with_tls(ServerConfig)`, `local_addr`, `with_session(config)` (static routing by SessionID), `with_dynamic_template(config)`, `allow_remotes(Vec<IpAddr>)`, `with_services`, `serve()`. Internal `Registry`.
- `connect_initiator(addr, config, app)`, `connect_initiator_with(...services)`, `connect_initiator_tls(addr, config, app, services, ClientConfig, ServerName)` (FR-F6), `connect_initiator_reconnecting(addr, config, app, services)->ReconnectHandle` (auto-reconnect on `reconnect_interval`).
- `ReconnectHandle { stop: Arc<AtomicBool>, task }` — `stop()`, `join()`.
- Private `run_connection`/`drain_messages`/`dispatch`/`route_and_run`/`dynamic_config`: drive `Session`, restore+persist seq numbers via store, log in/out, app callbacks (to_admin/to_app/from_admin[from_admin Err tears down]/from_app), monitor updates. **Comment:** "Garbled frames are dropped at this stage; RejectGarbledMessage arrives in S3" (minor deferred item).
- `framing.rs`: `frame_length(buf)->Result<Option<usize>, DecodeError>` using BodyLength + fixed 7-byte `10=XXX<SOH>` trailer. Inline tests: frames_complete_message, needs_more_bytes, detects_two_messages_in_buffer. **Fully implemented.**

# 5. truefix-config (`.cfg` settings parsing)
Files: `src/lib.rs` only.
**Public types & API:**
- `ConfigError` {Malformed{line,text}, UnresolvedVariable{line,name}}.
- `SessionSettings { default: BTreeMap<String,String>, sessions: Vec<BTreeMap<String,String>> }` — `parse(&str)`, `default_section()`, `sessions()`.
- Parses `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` dialect: inheritance (sessions merge defaults, per-session override), `#` comments (full-line + trailing), `${name}` interpolation. **Does NOT recognize specific Appendix A keys yet** — stores arbitrary key=value pairs generically; full key surface + mapping to SessionConfig deferred (Stage S2 comment). Inline tests: default_inherited_and_session_overrides, variable_interpolation, comments_and_blank_lines_ignored, unresolved_variable_errors, malformed_line_errors.

# 6. truefix-store (persistence)
Files: `src/{lib,memory,file,noop,sql}.rs`. Feature `sql` (off by default) gates sql.rs + `SqlStore`.
**Public types & API:**
- `StoreError` {Io(String), Backend(String)}.
- `MessageStore` async trait: `next_sender_seq/next_target_seq`, `set_next_sender_seq/set_next_target_seq`, `save(seq, &[u8])`, `get(begin,end)->Vec<(u64,Vec<u8>)>`, `reset()`.
- `StoreConfig` enum {Memory, File{dir}, CachedFile{dir}, Noop, #[cfg(sql)] Sql{url}}.
- `build_store(config)->Box<dyn MessageStore>` (async).
- `MemoryStore` (Mutex<State{sender,target,messages: BTreeMap}>) — **fully implemented**. `State` and `range()` are `pub(crate)` (shared by FileStore).
- `FileStore` — durable; `seqnums` file (sender\n target\n) + `body` append-log records `seq(8 LE)|len(4 LE)|bytes`; replays on open, **tolerates/recover truncation and flags `was_corrupted()`** (ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore, T060). **Fully implemented.**
- `CachedFileStore(FileStore)` — wraps FileStore; doc says write-batching optimizations deferred to S5 (currently shares FileStore impl). `was_corrupted()` exposed.
- `NoopStore` (unit struct) — seqs always 1, saves discarded.
- `SqlStore` (sqlx SQLite) — tables `seqnums(id=0, sender, target)` + `messages(seq PK, data BLOB)`; `connect` creates schema; all trait methods implemented. **Fully implemented.**

# 7. truefix-log (logging)
Files: `src/{lib,screen,file,composite,tracing_log}.rs`.
**Public types & API:**
- `LogError` {Io(String)}.
- `Log` trait (Send+Sync): `on_incoming(&str)`, `on_outgoing(&str)`, `on_event(&str)` (infallible, best-effort).

Files: `src/lib.rs` only. Pure re-exports: `truefix_core::{decode,encode,Field,FieldError,FieldMap,Group,Message,MessageCracker,MessageFactory,DecodeError}`, `truefix_session::{Action,Application,Event,Role,Session,SessionConfig,SessionId,SessionState}`, `truefix_transport::{connect_initiator,Acceptor,SessionHandle}`, `truefix_config as config`. I read every `src/**/*.rs` source file (plus all `Cargo.toml`s and `truefix-dict/build.rs`) across all 9 crates under `crates/`. What I did NOT read in detail: the `tests/**/*.rs` files and fixture `.fix` files — I identified them by name from the glob only, because the budget turned critical before I could read them. Findings below are from the actual source.

# Workspace (root `Cargo.toml`)
- `members = ["crates/*"]`, `resolver = "2"`, edition 2021, rust-version 1.96, `unsafe_code = "forbid"` (global workspace lint).
- Shared deps (`[workspace.dependencies]`): tokio(full), tokio-rustls 0.26, rustls 0.23, rustls-pemfile 2, thiserror 2, rust_decimal 1, time 0.3, serde 1, sqlx 0.8 (runtime-tokio), tracing 0.1, metrics 0.24, async-trait 0.1, plus path deps for all truefix-* crates. No new crates vs. directory listing (9 members). `truefix-at` has NO dependencies (empty scaffold).

## Per-crate lint policy
Every lib.rs uses `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(clippy::unwrap_used, expect_used, panic, indexing_slicing))]`. No `unimplemented!()` macros and no literal `TODO:` comments anywhere — deferral is expressed as prose ("arrives in later stages"). I found NO panicking/placeholder code in production paths; the only "skeletons" are trait contracts (see below).

---

# 1. truefix-core (codec/field/message model)
Files: `src/{lib,message,field,field_map,group,tags,error,cracker,factory}.rs`, `src/codec/{mod,encode,decode}.rs`.
**Public types & API:**
- `Field { tag, value }` — `new/string/int`, accessors `tag()`, `value_bytes()`, `as_str/as_int/as_decimal/as_char/as_bool/as_utc_timestamp()` (all return `Result<_, FieldError>`; never panic). `parse_utc_timestamp` handles up-to-picosecond fractions truncated to ns.
- `FieldMap` — ordered `members: Vec<Member>` where `Member = Field | Group{count_tag, entries}`. Methods: `add_field`, `set`, `get`, `contains`, `add_group`, `group`, `fields()`. `members()` is `pub(crate)`.
- `Group { count_tag, entries }` — `new`, `add_entry`, `count_tag/len/is_empty`, `into_parts` (pub(crate)).
- `Message { header, body, trailer: FieldMap }` — `new`, `begin_string()`, `msg_type()`, `encode()`, `decode(bytes)`.
- `MessageCracker` trait (skeleton): `type Error; fn crack(&mut self, &Message) -> Result<(), Error>`. Doc explicitly says full typed dispatch arrives at S4 — this is a contract skeleton, not implemented logic.
- `MessageFactory` trait (skeleton): `fn create(&self, begin_string, msg_type) -> Message`. Doc says dictionary-backed impl arrives in truefix-dict (S4) — contract skeleton.
- `error`: `FieldError` {NotUtf8, NotInt, NotDecimal, NotChar, NotBool, NotTimestamp}; `DecodeError` {Empty, MissingBeginString, InvalidBodyLength, BodyLengthMismatch{declared,actual}, MissingChecksum, ChecksumMismatch{declared,computed}, GarbledField{offset,reason}, Truncated{offset}, InvalidTag{offset}}.
- `tags`: constants SOH/BEGIN_STRING(8)/BODY_LENGTH(9)/MSG_TYPE(35)/CHECK_SUM(10)/MSG_SEQ_NUM(34)/SENDER_COMP_ID(49)/TARGET_COMP_ID(56)/SENDING_TIME(52); `is_header(tag)`, `is_trailer(tag)`, `data_field_for_length(tag)->Option<u32>` (maps length→data tag pairs incl. 95→96, 90→91, 212→213, 34x→34x+1 encoded-* pairs).
- `codec::encode` — canonical order `8,9,35,<rest header>,<body>,<trailer except 10>,10`; computes BodyLength + CheckSum (mod 256); recursively renders groups (count tag = entries.len()).
- `codec::decode` — tokenizes `tag=value<SOH>` honoring length-prefixed binary data fields; verifies BeginString/BodyLength/CheckSum; routes fields to header/body/trailer via `is_header`/`is_trailer`. **Fully implemented, flat-field (no dict group parsing at this layer).**

# 2. truefix-dict (dual-track data dictionary)
Files: `src/{lib,model,parser,validate,fixt,hash}.rs`, `build.rs`, `dict-src/normalized/*.fixdict` (FIX40/41/42/43/44, FIXT11, FIX50/50SP1/50SP2).
**Public types & API:**
- `FieldType` enum {Int,Length,SeqNum,NumInGroup,Float,Price,Qty,Amt,Percentage,Char,Boolean,String,Data,UtcTimestamp,UtcTimeOnly,UtcDateOnly,MonthYear} — `parse(token)`, `value_ok(&Field)->bool`.
- `FieldDef { tag, name, field_type, values: Vec<String> }` — `allows(&str)->bool`.
- `MessageDef { msg_type, name, required: Vec<u32>, optional: Vec<u32> }` — `allows_tag(u32)`.
- `DataDictionary` (fields private `pub(crate)`: version, fields, field_by_name, messages, header, trailer, hash) — accessors `version()`, `hash()`, `field(tag)`, `field_by_name(name)`, `message(msg_type)`, `is_header/is_trailer(tag)`, `field_count/message_count`.
- `RejectReason` enum (codes: InvalidTagNumber=0, RequiredTagMissing=1, TagNotDefinedForMessageType=2, UndefinedTag=3, TagSpecifiedWithoutValue=4, ValueIsIncorrect=5, IncorrectDataFormat=6, InvalidMsgType=11) — `code()->u32`.
- `ValidationError { reason, ref_tag, business: bool, text }` (thiserror) — `session`/`business` constructors are `pub(crate)`.
- `ValidationOptions { validate_fields_have_values=true, validate_user_defined_fields=false, allow_unknown_msg_fields=false, check_required_fields=true, check_field_types=true }` — maps to FIX `Validate*` toggles.
- `FixtDictionaries { transport, applications: BTreeMap, default_appl_ver_id }` — `new`, `with_application`, `with_default_appl_ver_id`, `transport()`, `application_for(Option<&str>)`.
- `parser::parse(&str)->Result<DataDictionary, ParseError>` — parses directives `version`, `field`, `header`, `trailer`, `message` (with `req:`/`opt:` tag lists); `ParseError` {MissingVersion, Malformed{line,reason}, UnknownType{line,token}}. Comments via `#`. **Fully implemented.**
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate(&Message, &ValidationOptions)->Result<(), ValidationError>` — checks empty values, UDF handling (tags≥5000), field-type/format/enum, tag-membership, required-fields; returns session-level reject vs business-level reject (InvalidMsgType). **Fully implemented.**
- `hash.rs` + `build.rs`: FNV-1a 64-bit (duplicated in both for codegen/runtime parity). `build.rs` generates `generated.rs` with `*_DICT_HASH: u64` constants + `pub mod <version>_msgs { pub const MSGNAME: &str }` per version, scanned from `message` directives.
- `lib.rs` re-exports + `include!(generated.rs)` + bundled dict sources (`FIX40_DICT`..`FIX50SP2_DICT` const strs) + `load_fixXX()` fns + `ALL_DICTS` table. **Fully implemented, no stubs.**

# 3. truefix-session (sans-IO state machine)
Files: `src/{lib,state,config,schedule,admin,tags,application,time_util,session_id}.rs`.
**Public types & API:**
- `SessionState` enum {Disconnected, AwaitingLogon, LoggedOn, AwaitingLogout}.
- `Event` enum {Connected, Received(Message), Tick, StartLogout}.
- `Action` enum {Send(Message), Disconnect}.
- `SessionStatus { state, logged_on, next_sender_seq, next_target_seq }` (monitoring snapshot, FR-L1).
- `Session` (struct, private fields incl. `store: BTreeMap<u64,Message>` in-memory sent msgs, `queue: BTreeMap<u64,Message>` out-of-order, `validator: Option<(DataDictionary, ValidationOptions)>`). Methods: `new(config)`, `set_dictionary(dict, opts)`, `id()`, `state()`, `is_logged_on()`, `next_out_seq/next_in_seq()`, `seed_sequences(out,in)`, `status()`, `reset()`, `handle(Event)->Vec<Action>`, `send_app(Message)->Vec<Action>`. **Heavily implemented (S3):** logon (incl. ResetSeqNumFlag + NextExpectedMsgSeqNum 789), heartbeat/test-request, logout, latency check (CheckLatency/MaxLatency → logout on stale SendingTime), inbound seq validation (Equal/Greater[queue+ResendRequest]/Less[PossDup ignore or logout]), sequence-number accounting on Logon, ResendRequest (35=2) handling with chunked `resend_request_chunk_size`, SequenceReset (35=4) gap-fill vs reset, `drain_queue`, app-message validation hook producing Reject (35=3) / BusinessMessageReject (35=j), `prepare_resend` stamps PossDup+OrigSendingTime. Admin types 0/1/2/3/4/5/A excluded from validation.
- `admin.rs` (pub(crate)): builders `logon/logout/heartbeat/test_request/resend_request/sequence_reset/reject/reject_with_reason/business_message_reject/prepare_resend` — **fully implemented.**
- `config.rs`: `Role` enum {Initiator, Acceptor}; `SessionConfig` struct with fields: begin_string, sender_comp_id, target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval(=30), role, reset_on_logon(=true), reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size(=0), enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num, check_latency(=true), max_latency(=120), logon_timeout(=10), logout_timeout(=10), reconnect_interval(=5), schedule: Option<Schedule>. `new()` + `session_id()`.
- `schedule.rs`: `Schedule { non_stop, start_time: Option<Time>, end_time, weekdays: Option<Vec<Weekday>>, utc_offset_seconds }` — `non_stop()`, `daily(start,end)`, `with_weekdays`, `with_utc_offset_seconds`, `is_in_session(now_utc)` (handles midnight wrap). **Implemented (simplified TZ, no StartDay/EndDay yet).**
- `application.rs`: `Application` async trait (on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app) with no-op defaults; from_admin/from_app return `Result<(),String>`.
- `session_id.rs`: `SessionId` (begin_string, sender/target comp+sub+location ids, session_qualifier) — `new()`, `Display` as `begin:sender->target[:qualifier]`.
- `tags.rs` (pub(crate)): MSG_SEQ_NUM(34), POSS_DUP_FLAG(43), SENDING_TIME(52), ORIG_SENDING_TIME(122), NEW_SEQ_NO(36), GAP_FILL_FLAG(123), BEGIN_SEQ_NO(7), END_SEQ_NO(16), RESET_SEQ_NUM_FLAG(141), NEXT_EXPECTED_MSG_SEQ_NUM(789), TEST_REQ_ID(112), TEXT(58), REF_SEQ_NUM(45), REF_MSG_TYPE(372), SESSION_REJECT_REASON(373), BUSINESS_REJECT_REASON(380), ENCRYPT_METHOD(98), HEART_BT_INT(108).
- Inline tests in `time_util.rs`: `format_has_expected_shape`.

# 4. truefix-transport (tokio TCP/TLS initiator+acceptor)
Files: `src/{lib,framing}.rs`.
**Public types & API:**
- `SocketOptions { tcp_no_delay }` — `apply(&TcpStream)`.
- `Services { store: Option<Arc<dyn MessageStore>>, log: Option<Arc<dyn Log>>, socket_options, monitor: Option<Monitor> }` (Clone/Default).
- `Monitor` (FR-L): `new`, `status(id)`, `is_connected(id)`, `sessions()`, `force_logout(id)`, `reset(id)` (async). Internal `MonitorEntry { status, connected, control }`.
- `SessionHandle { control, task }` — `logout()`, `join()`.
- `Acceptor<A>` (single static config) — `bind/bind_with`, `local_addr()`, `serve()->JoinHandle`.
- `AcceptorBuilder<A>` (multi-session) — `bind`, `with_tls(ServerConfig)`, `local_addr`, `with_session(config)` (static routing by SessionID), `with_dynamic_template(config)`, `allow_remotes(Vec<IpAddr>)`, `with_services`, `serve()`. Internal `Registry`.
- `connect_initiator(addr, config, app)`, `connect_initiator_with(...services)`, `connect_initiator_tls(addr, config, app, services, ClientConfig, ServerName)` (FR-F6), `connect_initiator_reconnecting(addr, config, app, services)->ReconnectHandle` (auto-reconnect on `reconnect_interval`).
- `ReconnectHandle { stop: Arc<AtomicBool>, task }` — `stop()`, `join()`.
- Private `run_connection`/`drain_messages`/`dispatch`/`route_and_run`/`dynamic_config`: drive `Session`, restore+persist seq numbers via store, log in/out, app callbacks (to_admin/to_app/from_admin[from_admin Err tears down]/from_app), monitor updates. **Comment:** "Garbled frames are dropped at this stage; RejectGarbledMessage arrives in S3" (minor deferred item).
- `framing.rs`: `frame_length(buf)->Result<Option<usize>, DecodeError>` using BodyLength + fixed 7-byte `10=XXX<SOH>` trailer. Inline tests: frames_complete_message, needs_more_bytes, detects_two_messages_in_buffer. **Fully implemented.**

# 5. truefix-config (`.cfg` settings parsing)
Files: `src/lib.rs` only.
**Public types & API:**
- `ConfigError` {Malformed{line,text}, UnresolvedVariable{line,name}}.
- `SessionSettings { default: BTreeMap<String,String>, sessions: Vec<BTreeMap<String,String>> }` — `parse(&str)`, `default_section()`, `sessions()`.
- Parses `[DEFAULT]`/`[SESSION]` dialect: inheritance (sessions merge defaults, per-session override), `#` comments (full-line + trailing), `${name}` interpolation. **Does NOT recognize specific Appendix A keys yet** — stores arbitrary key=value pairs generically; full key surface + mapping to SessionConfig deferred (Stage S2 comment). Inline tests: default_inherited_and_session_overrides, variable_interpolation, comments_and_blank_lines_ignored, unresolved_variable_errors, malformed_line_errors.

# 6. truefix-store (persistence)
Files: `src/{lib,memory,file,noop,sql}.rs`. Feature `sql` (off by default) gates sql.rs + `SqlStore`.
**Public types & API:**
- `StoreError` {Io(String), Backend(String)}.
- `MessageStore` async trait: `next_sender_seq/next_target_seq`, `set_next_sender_seq/set_next_target_seq`, `save(seq, &[u8])`, `get(begin,end)->Vec<(u64,Vec<u8>)>`, `reset()`.
- `StoreConfig` enum {Memory, File{dir}, CachedFile{dir}, Noop, #[cfg(sql)] Sql{url}}.
- `build_store(config)->Box<dyn MessageStore>` (async).
- `MemoryStore` (Mutex<State{sender,target,messages: BTreeMap}>) — **fully implemented**. `State` and `range()` are `pub(crate)` (shared by FileStore).
- `FileStore` — durable; `seqnums` file (sender\n target\n) + `body` append-log records `seq(8 LE)|len(4 LE)|bytes`; replays on open, **tolerates/recover truncation and flags `was_corrupted()`** (ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore, T060). **Fully implemented.**
- `CachedFileStore(FileStore)` — wraps FileStore; doc says write-batching optimizations deferred to S5 (currently shares FileStore impl). `was_corrupted()` exposed.
- `NoopStore` (unit struct) — seqs always 1, saves discarded.
- `SqlStore` (sqlx SQLite) — tables `seqnums(id=0, sender, target)` + `messages(seq PK, data BLOB)`; `connect` creates schema; all trait methods implemented. **Fully implemented.**

# 7. truefix-log (logging)
Files: `src/{lib,screen,file,composite,tracing_log}.rs`.
**Public types & API:**
- `LogError` {Io(String)}.
- `Log` trait (Send+Sync): `on_incoming(&str)`, `on_outgoing(&str)`, `on_event(&str)` (infallible, best-effort).
- `LogConfig` enum {Screen, File{dir}, Tracing, Composite(Vec<LogConfig>)}.

Pure re-exports: `truefix_core::{decode,encode,Field,FieldError,FieldMap,Group,Message,MessageCracker,MessageFactory,DecodeError}`, `truefix_session::{Action,Application,Event,Role,Session,SessionConfig,SessionId,SessionState}`, `truefix_transport::{connect_initiator,Acceptor,SessionHandle}`, `truefix_config as config`. functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task

Call this tool to submit your final result and complete the task. This is the ONLY way to finish.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
- Choose a status that matches the outcome: use 'success' for fully completed work, 'partial' for meaningful progress with straightforward continuation, 'blocked' for external blockers that you cannot resolve autonomously, and 'failure' for invalid or unrecoverable paths

## Per-crate lint policy
Every lib.rs uses `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(clippy::unwrap_used, expect_used, panic, indexing_slicing))]`. No `unimplemented!()` macros and no literal `TODO:` comments anywhere — deferral is expressed as prose ("arrives in later stages"). I found NO panicking/placeholder code in production paths; the only "skeletons" are trait contracts (see below).
